title: baccaurea griffoniana
intro: Baccaurea griffoniana is a species of plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, commonly known as the 'Mountain Apple' due to its edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Baccaurea griffoniana include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruit are often used to make teas, infusions, and jams, which are consumed for their medicinal and nutritional benefits.
